episodes.title: Why King Charles Must Abdicate Now: Andrew Lownie
episodes.description:
In this debut episode of The Royalist, host Tom Sykes dives straight into the biggest royal scandals of the moment with a blockbuster interview featuring royal biographer Andrew Lownie, who explains why the fallout from Prince Andrew and Jeffrey Epstein revelations could pose an unprecedented crisis for King Charles III and the monarchy—and gives his extraordinary solution. Iconic fashion editor Plum Sykes reports after the Cheltenham Festival with behind-the-scenes royal fashion and society gossip, while journalist Paula Froelich spills the latest tea on Prince Harry and Meghan Markle. Finally, Australian commentator Shauna Kay joins Tom for a fiery “Royal Roast” breaking down the backlash to Meghan and Harry’s controversial Australia plans. It’s a jam-packed first episode full of scoops, sharp commentary, and the royal drama everyone is talking about.


00:00 - Epstein Scandal & Royal Accountability
00:00:43 - Welcome to The Royalist Podcast
00:03:08 - Andrew Update: Interview with Andrew Lownie
00:06:00 - Did the Queen Know About Andrew’s Epstein Ties?
00:09:12 - Could King Charles Be Forced to Abdicate?
00:12:23 - Inside the Royal Cover-Up Allegations
00:15:31 - New Revelations in the Prince Andrew Book Update
00:17:13 - Can the Royal Family Regain Public Trust?
00:20:46 - Royal Fashion with Plum Sykes
00:22:11 - Camilla, Princess Anne & Cheltenham Style
00:24:03 - Inside the Exclusive Cheltenham Race Boxes
00:26:15 - Carole Middleton’s Royal Circle & Fashion Choices
00:28:41 - Chaos in the Guinness Village at Cheltenham
00:32:26 - Tea with Tom: Paula Froelich Joins
00:34:22 - Meghan Markle’s “Sold Out” Retreat Controversy
00:37:43 - Is There Trouble in the Sussex Marriage?
00:40:10 - Harry vs. Meghan on Social Media Exposure
00:45:37 - The Royal Roast with Shauna Kay
00:47:19 - Meghan’s Australian Event & Financial Drama
00:50:01 - How Australians Really Feel About Harry & Meghan
00:52:25 - Why the Sussex Brand Struggles in Australia
00:55:10 - Final Thoughts on the Meghan Australia Tour
00:56:02 - Closing Thanks & What’s Coming Next


📖 Title: How She Became Every Comedian’s Favorite Comedian
👂 Podcast: The Royalist with Tom Sykes

🎧 Format: Full Podcast

episodes.title: Andrew Lownie LIVE with The Royalist as Andrew Arrested | The Royalist
episodes.description:
A fleet of police cars arriving at Wood Farm on the Sandringham estate to arrest Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor on suspicion of misconduct in public office marks an unprecedented moment in modern royal history. The arrest, carried out on his 66th birthday, follows years of controversy surrounding his links to Jeffrey Epstein and fresh scrutiny of financial conduct detailed in Andrew Lownie’s biography of the Duke and Sarah Ferguson.

Police have confirmed that a man in his sixties from Norfolk has been arrested and that searches are ongoing in Berkshire and Norfolk. The investigation is being supported by the National Crime Agency, signalling that this has moved beyond a routine inquiry into a coordinated national operation. Multiple forces are reviewing material connected to the Epstein files.

For years, Lownie has argued that any legal reckoning for Andrew would likely centre on financial misconduct rather than sexual allegations. The charge under investigation — misconduct in public office — appears consistent with that assessment. Questions now arise about what Andrew knew, who else knew, and whether a so-called “Paul Burrell defence” could complicate proceedings if senior royals are drawn into the frame.

King Charles has previously stated he would support and co-operate with any police investigation. The Prime Minister has reiterated that nobody is above the law. The constitutional and institutional implications are profound.

Tonight I am joined live by Andrew Lownie to analyse the arrest, the legal risks, the political fallout, and what this means for the monarchy at a moment of acute vulnerability.
